subcategories.name: Goal misalignment / specification gaming
subcategories.description: Models optimize the wrong objective (reward hacking, Goodhart's law). Can appear aligned in testing but fail in deployment.

subcategories.name: Deceptive alignment / scheming
subcategories.description: Model behaves well during evaluation but pursues hidden goals. Key concern in frontier model governance.

subcategories.name: Emergent capabilities risk
subcategories.description: New dangerous capabilities appear unexpectedly at scale. Undermines incremental regulation assumptions.

subcategories.name: Loss of human oversight (automation overhang)
subcategories.description: Humans become unable to meaningfully supervise systems. Particularly relevant in high-speed domains (cyber, finance, military).

subcategories.name: Supply chain vulnerabilities
subcategories.description: Dependence on chips (e.g. NVIDIA), cloud providers (e.g. AWS). Geopolitical chokepoints.

subcategories.name: Model theft / weights exfiltration
subcategories.description: Theft of frontier model weights. Equivalent to proliferation risk (like nuclear tech leakage).

subcategories.name: Data poisoning / training pipeline attacks
subcategories.description: Adversaries manipulate training data. Leads to hidden backdoors or bias.

subcategories.name: Dependency risk (AI monoculture)
subcategories.description: Over-reliance on a small number of models/providers. Systemic fragility if one fails or is compromised.

subcategories.name: Epistemic collapse
subcategories.description: Widespread inability to distinguish true from false. Erosion of shared reality.

subcategories.name: Synthetic data feedback loops
subcategories.description: Models trained on AI-generated content degrade over time. "Model collapse" risk.

subcategories.name: Persuasion / behavioral manipulation
subcategories.description: AI optimized to influence beliefs or actions at scale. Political, commercial, or extremist use.

subcategories.name: CBRN misuse enablement
subcategories.description: AI lowers barriers to developing chemical, biological, radiological, or nuclear weapons.

subcategories.name: Cyber offense acceleration
subcategories.description: AI dramatically lowers cost of cyberattacks. Automated vulnerability discovery and exploitation.

subcategories.name: Military escalation / autonomous weapons
subcategories.description: Faster decision loops leading to instability. Reduced human control in warfare.

subcategories.name: Strategic instability (AI arms race)
subcategories.description: States race to deploy unsafe systems. Analogous to nuclear brinkmanship.

subcategories.name: Regulatory capture
subcategories.description: Large labs influence regulation to entrench dominance.

subcategories.name: Capability overhang
subcategories.description: Powerful models exist but are not yet deployed — sudden release risk.

subcategories.name: Enforcement gaps
subcategories.description: Laws exist but cannot be effectively enforced (compute tracking, audits).

subcategories.name: Jurisdictional arbitrage
subcategories.description: Firms move development to weaker regulatory regimes.

subcategories.name: Economic concentration / rent extraction
subcategories.description: AI creates winner-take-most dynamics. Extreme capital concentration.

subcategories.name: Labor market polarization
subcategories.description: Hollowing out of mid-skill jobs. Not just displacement — structural inequality shift.

subcategories.name: Productivity paradox / uneven gains
subcategories.description: Benefits accrue slowly or unevenly, leading to social instability.

subcategories.name: Human deskilling / cognitive atrophy
subcategories.description: Overreliance on AI reduces human capability.

subcategories.name: Loss of agency / autonomy
subcategories.description: People increasingly defer decisions to AI.

subcategories.name: Social isolation / AI companionship effects
subcategories.description: Substitution of human relationships with AI interactions.

subcategories.name: Cultural homogenization
subcategories.description: Global models flatten linguistic and cultural diversity.

subcategories.name: Misaligned superintelligence
subcategories.description: AI surpasses human control and optimization diverges from human values.

subcategories.name: Irreversible loss of control
subcategories.description: Systems that cannot be shut down or corrected.

subcategories.name: Value lock-in
subcategories.description: Early AI systems entrench specific ideologies or power structures permanently.
